subject: Add the executor and the component function to support 5G NRM CM
commit_message:
Add the executor and the component function to support 5G NRM CM

The executor and the component function are designed to invoke the 5G NRM CM opertions: createMOI getMOIAttributes modifyMOIAttributes and deleteMOI

Issue-ID: CCSDK-2003
Signed-off-by: Huang Cheng <duke.huangcheng@huawei.com>
Change-Id: I20d3e97c01f6499e73ab0ec77abbc24775f428b7

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

You should not be using Jython, jython is on the path of being completely removed for G release. I would not suggest creating something new that uses it.

Either you change the implementation to use Kotlin or make use of py-executor

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2

1) Jython shouldn't be used
2) what benifit restful-executore is adding, we have rest-lib and same can be used to perform all actions
3) if really required a wrapper on top of restlib, it can be part of CBA script itself.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> You should not be using Jython, jython is on the path of being completely removed for G release. I would not suggest creating something new that uses it.
> 
> Either you change the implementation to use Kotlin or make use of py-executor

Hello Sebastien
Thank you for your comments. I will fix it ASAP

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2
> 
> 1) Jython shouldn't be used
> 2) what benifit restful-executore is adding, we have rest-lib and same can be used to perform all actions
> 3) if really required a wrapper on top of restlib, it can be part of CBA script itself.

Hello Kapil

Thank you for your comment. I will fix the Jython problem ASAP.
About your question, maybe the "restful-executor" leads to some misunderstanding. This executor and the corresponding functions are designed for the restful 5G NRM configuration management scenario. With this executor and the functions, the uploaded blueprint can simply perform the 5G NRM configuration management operations (create the MOIs, get the MOI attributes, modify the MOI attributes, and delete the MOIs) by invoking different workflows.

If you have questions, please let me know.

BR

Huang cheng

Patch Set 8:

How restful-executor is generic ? and why it can't be merged with nrm-restful ?

Patch Set 8: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ccsdk-cds-maven-verify-master-mvn35-openjdk8/369/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 8:

> Patch Set 8:
> 
> How restful-executor is generic ? and why it can't be merged with nrm-restful ?

Hello KAPIL

Thank you for your comments and help. For your questions:

1. The restful-executor is designed to invoke different workflows (config-deploy, config-get, config-modify, and config-delete) in different 5G NRM CM scenarios. In the different workflows, different nrm-restful functions (create MOIs, get MOI attributes, modify MOI attributes, and delete MOI) will be invoked to generate the corresponding restful request and send them to EMS. The restful-executor will merge the response and report them to SO.  The restful-executor is capable to support all four operations in the 5G NRM CM lifecycle and the configuration management of multiple or single 5G NRM MOIs (management object instances).

2. When I present the implementation plan to CDS team, I get one suggestion that it will be better to separate the nrm-restful function code from the executor code. So I create two folders (nrm-restful and restful-executor) under the directory (root/ms/blueprintsprocessor/functions). It will be easy for some other projects which want to invoke nrm-related operation in the future. There is a plan B. I will only create one folder under the directory (root/ms/blueprintsprocessor/functions). Inside the folder, I will create two separated folders. One for the nrm-restful, another for the restful-executor. I can upload another patch set for plan B. Then, we can decide which patch set is better.

If anyone has any advice or other solutions, I'm all ears.

Thank you for your comments and help.

BR

Huang cheng
